name: Zottman Curl
resistance_profile: Curl up supinated to load the biceps, rotate to a pronated grip at the top, then lower under control to hammer the brachialis and forearm extensors on the eccentric.
cues: Curl up with palms up, rotate to palms down at the top
Lower slowly in the pronated grip
Keep the elbows pinned to your sides throughout
execution_steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward and elbows at your sides.
Curl the dumbbells up with a supinated grip until they reach shoulder height.
At the top, rotate your wrists so your palms face down into a pronated grip.
Lower the dumbbells slowly under control in the pronated position to full extension.
Rotate the palms back to supinated at the bottom and repeat.
good_when: You want to train the biceps and forearm flexors/extensors in one movement
Building grip and elbow-flexor balance with minimal equipment
You want extra eccentric emphasis on the brachialis
bad_when: You want to maximize biceps-specific load — the pronated lowering caps the weight
Wrist rotation under load bothers your elbows or wrists
common_errors: Rushing the pronated eccentric that gives the exercise its value
Swinging the torso to move the weight
progressions: Slow the pronated lowering to a 3-4 second count
regressions: Perform standard alternating dumbbell curls first
